resources.string: Moonlight is testing your network connection to determine if any required ports are blocked.\n\nThis may take a few seconds…

Your network does not appear to be blocking Moonlight. If you still have trouble connecting, check your PC\'s firewall settings.\n\nIf you are trying to stream over the Internet, install the Moonlight Internet Hosting Tool on your PC and run the included Internet Streaming Tester to check your PC\'s Internet connection.
The network test could not be performed because none of Moonlight\'s connection testing servers were reachable. Check your Internet connection or try again later.

Your device's current network connection seems to be blocking Moonlight. Streaming over the Internet may not work while connected to this network.

The following network ports were blocked:
Your device\'s current network connection is blocking Moonlight. Streaming over the Internet may not work while connected to this network.
Computer is currently in a game. You must close the game before pairing.
Please enter the following PIN on the target PC:
If your host PC is running Sunshine, navigate to the Sunshine web UI to enter the PIN.
Unable to wake PC because there is no stored MAC address
It may take a few seconds for your PC to wake up. If it doesn\'t, make sure it\'s configured properly for Wake-On-LAN.
The ComputerManager service is not running. Please wait a few seconds or restart the app.
GFE returned an HTTP 404 error. Make sure your PC is running a supported GPU. Using remote desktop software can also cause this error. Try rebooting your machine or reinstalling GFE.
Moonlight has crashed due to an incompatibility with this device\'s video decoder. Try adjusting the streaming settings if the crashes continue.
Your device\'s video decoder continues to crash at your selected streaming settings. Your streaming settings have been reset to default.
USB access is prohibited by your device administrator. Check your Knox or MDM settings.
Your current launcher does not allow for creating pinned shortcuts.
Video decoder failed to initialize. Your device may not support the selected resolution or frame rate.
Your network connection isn\'t performing well. Reduce your video bitrate setting or try a faster connection.
Something went wrong on your host PC when starting the stream.\n\nMake sure you don\'t have any DRM-protected content open on your host PC. You can also try restarting your host PC.
The host PC reported a fatal video encoding error.\n\nTry disabling HDR mode, changing the streaming resolution, or changing your host PC\'s display resolution.
Check your firewall and port forwarding rules for port(s):
Warning: Your active network connection is metered!
Searching for host PCs on your local network…\n\n Ensure Sunshine is running on your host PC or GameStream is enabled in the GeForce Experience SHIELD settings.
Are you sure you want to delete this PC?
Slow connection to PC\nReduce your bitrate
Incoming frame rate from network: %1$.2f FPS
Frames dropped by your network connection: %1$.2f%%
Are you sure you want to quit the running app? All unsaved data will be lost.
Unable to connect to the specified computer. Make sure the required ports are allowed through the firewall.
Unable to resolve PC address. Make sure you didn\'t make a typo in the address.
You must enter an IP address
That address doesn\'t look right. You must use your router\'s public IP address for streaming over the Internet.
Increase to improve image clarity. Decrease for better performance on lower end devices and slower networks.

Native resolution and/or FPS may not be supported by the streaming server. You will probably need to configure a matching custom display mode for the host PC manually.

If you choose to create a custom resolution in NVIDIA Control Panel to match your screen settings, please ensure you have read and understood NVIDIA's warning regarding possible monitor damage, PC instability, and other potential problems.

We are not responsible for any problems resulting from creating a custom resolution on your PC.

It may be that your monitor does not support a necessary display configuration. If so, you may try to set up a virtual monitor. Finally, if your device or host PC does not support streaming at a specific resolution or refresh rate, you're out of luck unfortunately.
Increase for a smoother video stream. Decrease for better performance on lower end devices.
Increase for better image quality. Decrease to improve performance on slower connections.
Enable 5.1 or 7.1 surround sound for home-theater systems
Allows audio effects to function while streaming, but may increase audio latency
Unchecking this option forces a gamepad to always be present
Vibrates your device to emulate rumble if your gamepad does not support it
Amplify or reduce the vibration intensity on your device
Note: Some games can enforce a larger deadzone than what Moonlight is configured to use.
Enables a built-in USB driver for devices without native Xbox controller support
Use Moonlight\'s USB driver for all supported gamepads, even if native Xbox、PS4、PS5、NS Pro controller support is present
Long pressing the Start button will switch the gamepad into mouse mode
Switches the face buttons A/B and X/Y for gamepads and the on-screen controls
Forces gamepad touchpad input to control the host mouse, even when emulating a gamepad with a touchpad.
Allow use of gamepad motion sensors
Enables supported hosts to request motion sensor data when emulating a gamepad with motion sensors. Disabling may slightly reduce power and network usage if motion sensors are not being used in game.

Uses your device\'s built-in motion sensors if gamepad sensors are not supported by your connected gamepad or your Android version.\nNote: Enabling this option may cause your gamepad to appear as a PlayStation controller on the host.
Use the touchscreen as a trackpad
If enabled, the touchscreen acts like a trackpad. If disabled, the touchscreen directly controls the mouse cursor.
This can make mouse acceleration behave more naturally for remote desktop usage, but it is incompatible with many games.
Enable back and forward mouse buttons
Enabling this option may break right clicking on some buggy devices
Show virtual controller overlay on touchscreen
Vibrates your device to emulate rumble for the on-screen controls
Hide all virtual buttons except L3 and R3
Show the guide button on screen
Resets all on-screen controls to their default size and position
Are you sure you want to delete your saved on-screen controls layout?
Make the on-screen controls more/less transparent
Allows the stream to be viewed (but not controlled) while multitasking
Smaller box art in the app grid allows more apps to be visible on screen
Allow GFE to modify game settings for optimal streaming
Play audio from the computer and this device
Microphone uplink requires Android 10 or a device with an Opus encoder. Streaming will continue without microphone uplink.
Microphone permission was denied. Streaming will continue without microphone uplink.
Streaming at 90 or 120 FPS may reduce latency on high-end devices but can cause lag or instability on devices that can\'t support it
Lower display refresh rates can save power at the expense of some additional video latency
Disable on-screen connection warning messages while streaming
May reduce micro-stuttering on some devices, but can increase latency
Newer codecs can lower video bandwidth requirements if your device supports them. Codec selections may be ignored if not supported by the host software or GPU.
Stream HDR when the game and PC GPU support it. HDR requires a GPU with HEVC Main 10 encoding support.
This will cause loss of detail in light and dark areas if your device doesn\'t properly display full range video content.
Display real-time stream performance information while streaming
Display a latency information message after the stream ends
View instructions on how to set up your gaming PC for streaming
View tips for diagnosing and fixing common streaming issues
Gamepad type may be changed due to motion sensor emulation
Specify how to balance video latency and smoothness
Prefer smoothest video (may significantly increase latency)

This feature is only used when an external physical keyboard is connected. Certain systems have default keyboard shortcuts that interfere with the streaming session by preventing key commands from being sent to the host PC. 

Moonlight Axixi does not monitor your text input, nor does it collect passwords, credit card details, or any other personal information. 

Follow us on Bilibili: Axixi's Daily Life
Send Win + X -> U -> U (Shut down)
Send Win + X -> U -> I (Sign out)
Send Win + G (Open Xbox Game Bar)
Send Ctrl + Alt + Shift + Q (Force host exit)
Send Ctrl + Alt + Shift + F1 (Switch host monitor)
Send Ctrl + Alt + Shift + F12 (Switch host monitor)
Use an analog stick to scroll
Select an analog stick to scroll when in mouse emulation mode

Tips:
1. Enable the NVIDIA GeForce Shield service on the host PC, or install Sunshine and enable its streaming service.
 2. If using the default port (47989), you don't need to specify it manually. Custom ports must be appended to the IP address (e.g., IP:port).
 3. IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are supported, for example:
 IPv4: 192.168.1.123
 IPv6: [fd00:6868:6868:0:fd6:e0ea:84d9:70ea]:47989
1. The controller is working on the test screen, but is not recognized in the streamed game.

Most likely, the virtual controller driver (ViGEmBus) is not installed on the host PC. Check if there are any warnings/errors on the Sunshine configuration page (Web UI). You can click "Cloud Drive Resources" to find the installer.\nOr, the controller might be identified as a DS4 controller, which is not supported by some games. You can resolve this by changing the controller type to xbox360 in Sunshine\'s configuration UI (under the Input tab).
2. How do I stream in full-screen? How do I play with the host screen turned off?

Use a virtual display or HDMI dummy plug to match your device\'s resolution.\n The HDMI dummy plug configuration must be created in your graphics card driver. Set it as the primary display in Windows display settings, as Sunshine captures the primary display by default (this default display can be changed in Sunshine\'s Web UI).\n To turn off the physical monitor while streaming, use Win + P or Windows display settings to select "Show only on display X" (the virtual one), or use the built-in headless display option in some newer Sunshine versions (e.g., Sunshine Apollo edition). Click "Cloud Drive Resources" to find relevant installation files.
Connect to your computer anytime, anywhere!
Thank you to every friend who supports and joins us on this journey!
Long press to open context menu for more options
Click refresh to update controller info.\nFor USB controller testing, enable "Override native gamepad support" in settings.
Thank you for walking with us all the way
This list is dedicated to every friend who supports Axixi. Because of you, this project can keep moving forward.
Tap Apply in the top right to apply settings! (Must be run manually on each connection)
The following settings may require reconnecting the controller to take effect:
The following settings require restarting the stream to take effect:
Tap Apply in top right to save configuration! (Run manually for each connection)
Applying settings will close this menu. Restart the stream to apply changes.
Codec selection may be ignored if the host software or GPU does not support it.
Boost window brightness to maximum in HDR mode. Off by default.
Supported on Snapdragon 8 Gen 1+ and MediaTek Dimensity 9300 or earlier chips.
Select the target resolution for FSR Super Resolution. HDR is handled automatically by the render pipeline.
Works with Sunshine (Axixi Edition) and Razer services. Compatible with Apollo expansion mode.
Required on some operating systems (e.g. ColorOS) to correctly set resolution/refresh rate.
Play audio on device and PC
Takes effect immediately. Keeps the game menu closed when the screen is locked.
Enter bitrate in Mbps (only validation for empty values is performed, please do not input invalid values)
Select an appropriate frame rate. Invalid values may result in a black screen.

Axixi Shortcuts: Prefix with AXIX, e.g., AXIX0 (soft keyboard), AXIX1 (virtual keys), AXIX2 (virtual full keyboard), AXIX3 (virtual controller), AXIX4 (floating button), AXIX5 (perf overlay), AXIX6 (game menu)
The following options are saved to the configuration file:
Controller Gyro Emulates Right Analog Stick (Beta)
Converts streaming audio to haptic feedback, best suited for explosions, collisions, and low-frequency effects.
Includes special optimizations for DualSense controllers connected in wired override mode.
Reduces vibration triggers from voices, chat, and announcers.
Settings are saved to storage and persist across sessions.
Mode Switch: Please enable the virtual controller first.
Mode Switch: Please enable the virtual keys first.
If key layouts are missing, click refresh in top right
Choose your preferred channel to download updates.
A new update is ready. Review the release notes below to download.
Click refresh to update controller info.\nFor USB controller testing, enable "Override native gamepad support" in settings.

If the slider control is difficult to adjust, you can enter the bitrate manually.\nBitrates exceeding the slider\'s maximum will not be displayed on the slider but will still take effect during streaming.
Please enter a number. Decimals are rounded to the nearest 0.5.
(Experimental) Supported on Snapdragon 8 Gen 1+ and MediaTek Dimensity 9300 or earlier chips.
Enable FSR Super Resolution to upscale the stream to 2K or 4K under GLES rendering.
Only takes effect when FSR upscaling is enabled. Higher values produce sharper edges.
SDR mapping is more stable. Native HDR output is experimental and requires device support for BT2020 PQ.
Useful for foldable screens or tablets. Customise the resolution and screen placement to your preference.
Use light theme on some pages
Enable this option if pulling the left trigger vibrates the right grip (and vice versa).
When checked, the controller trigger deadzone will not be restricted by Moonlight [except for some ASUS Gamepad devices].
Select a pointer input mode. Multi-touch requires Sunshine on the host PC.
By default, the cursor of an external physical mouse is captured. Enable this to display the Android system cursor.
Enable this option to get a multi-touch Windows experience similar to Microsoft Surface.\nRequires Sunshine 0.21 or above.
Landscape and portrait orientations support separate configurations.
Prevent virtual sticks from triggering L3/R3 click actions.
Disable center zone of virtual analog sticks
Select a layout file to override the current scheme (landscape mode only).
Export the configuration of the current scheme (landscape mode only).
Displays a simplified performance overlay: speed, network latency, decoding latency, packet loss, and FPS.
Lite HUD Click to Toggle Shortcut Menu
Tapping the Lite overlay opens the shortcut menu instead of triggering the back button.
Add extra stream metrics (resolution, HDR status, decoder, etc.) to the Lite overlay.
Set the top margin of the Lite HUD. Default is 4dp.
Set the top margin of the Lite HUD. Default is 4dp.
Show a shortcut menu when pressing the physical Back button during streaming (options for soft keyboard, performance overlay, mouse modes, and key macros).
Replaces the classic dialog menu with the modern UI overlay.
Enables SBS 3D mode (for entertainment only). Requires an external controller.
Restart the stream to apply the new resolution settings.

Add a custom resolution in WxH format (e.g. 1920x1080). Non-standard resolutions may fail to apply.\nFor notch screens, non-native aspect ratios may not automatically fill the cutout area. Use "Stretch video to full-screen" to force full screen.
To use with a custom resolution, make sure the streaming terminal you are using supports it and the PC terminal resolution is vertical.
Enable if Joy-Cons are unresponsive or have mapping issues. Supported on Android 10 or above.
Report controller battery status when available. \nDisable this if you experience stuttering when using touchscreen, trackpad, stylus, or mouse.
Enable a floating shortcut button to open the game menu quickly.
Check to enable key combination inputs by default on the full keyboard.
Displays the video stream on the external monitor while keeping virtual buttons, controls, and performance info on the phone screen.
Vibrate your phone instead of the controller if you prefer phone haptics. (No effect when using Moonlight\'s custom USB driver.)
Use Device Gyroscope for Virtual Controller

Allows the virtual controller to use the phone\'s internal gyroscope. You may need to set the emulated controller type to PS4 in Sunshine\'s Web UI. Recommended to disable when using a physical controller.
Disable the default shortcut macros if you prefer to only use your imported custom profile.
May prevent issues on Meta Quest running Android 13 or above.
Warning: This will overwrite your existing profile. Back it up first!
Displays key value overlays on the screen when pressing buttons in Moonlight. Helpful for creating custom mapping profiles.
Display overlay buttons (special function keys) on the touchscreen. Transparency is shared with the virtual controller.
Landscape and portrait layouts support independent configurations.
Vibrate the phone when tapping screen buttons (works for virtual controller and custom keys).
Select a configuration file to override the current virtual keys layout (landscape mode only).
Export the configuration of the current virtual keys layout (landscape mode only).
Converts streaming audio into haptic feedback, best suited for explosions, collisions, and low-frequency effects.
Output haptics to the phone or to a connected controller.
Adjust the haptic feedback intensity for your phone or controller.
Filter out human voices and chat audio to prevent unwanted haptic triggers.
Retain standard in-game controller rumble when haptics are set to the controller. Off by default.
Exports paired hosts data to a SQLite database (.db).
Imports hosts from a .db file. Overwrites the existing computer list (relaunch the app to apply).
Disable to use default background. Supported on Android 10 or above.
Select an image (rotate screen to apply).\nWarning: To prevent crashes, do not use excessively large image files. Clear app data if a crash loop occurs.
Apply a Gaussian blur effect to your custom background. Supported on Android 12 or above.
Skip the context menu and launch streaming immediately when tapping a game icon.
Version Info · Check Updates · Follow Author
Long press a game icon to open the options menu
